title: Disable goproxy
body:
GOPROXY is currently causing build issues like so:
```
go: github.com/containernetworking/cni@v0.7.1: GOPROXY list is not the empty string, but contains no entries
```
    
This commit sets `GOPROXY=direct` in rpm spec file.
    
Ref: https://download.copr.fedorainfracloud.org/results/packit/containers-podman-20627/centos-stream+epel-next-9-aarch64/06611633-podman/builder-live.log.gz
    
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]


<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL. This will fix the c9s rpm build issue.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> LGTM
> 
> Can you create a follow-up issue to revert the change once Google's proxy is back?

@vrothberg will do, but just wondering, do we even need it at all? Are speedups noticeable with it enabled? Also, where can I track GOPROXY status updates?

> Are speedups noticeable with it enabled?

Yes, the speedups are noticeable as we talk to the proxy instead of each repository.

> lso, where can I track GOPROXY status updates?

https://status.goproxy.cn/ indicates everything's OK though.
Should we close this PR then if GOPROXY is back up and running?

Maybe the better questions is why exactly do they reach to the network?? We vendor the dependencies so unless someone explicitly calls `make vendor` I don't see why this should contact the server at all.

> Maybe the better questions is why exactly do they reach to the network?? We vendor the dependencies so unless someone explicitly calls `make vendor` I don't see why this should contact the server at all.

looking at the failed build log, it's not downloading any modules but still using GOPROXY for whatever reason:

```
+ go build -buildmode pie -compiler gc '-tags=rpm_crashtraceback libtrust_openssl seccomp exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper systemd libsubid exclude_graphdriver_btrfs btrfs_noversion' -ldflags '-linkmode=external -compressdwarf=false  -B 0x1a78357ae3772691d99aafc9a2bb80bb450064ce -extldflags '\''-Wl,-z,relro -Wl,--as-needed  -Wl,-z,now -specs=/usr/lib/rpm/redhat/redhat-hardened-ld -specs=/usr/lib/rpm/redhat/redhat-annobin-cc1 '\''' -a -v -x -o bin/dnsname github.com/containers/dnsname/plugins/meta/dnsname
WORK=/tmp/go-build48391443
go: github.com/containernetworking/cni@v0.7.1: GOPROXY list is not the empty string, but contains no entries
error: Bad exit status from /var/tmp/rpm-tmp.8dR4Fz (%build)

```

The fact that this only fails on centos stream 9 clearly suggest that this is not a problem on the server side and instead something with the local toolchain, I guess it looks like https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-15361.

Anyway as workaround I good to merge this, AFAIK fedora set the goproxy to direct as default anyway so it should not make a difference.
LGTM

> Anyway as workaround I good to merge this, AFAIK fedora set the goproxy to direct as default anyway so it should not make a difference. LGTM

@Luap99 Thanks
